description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the python:3.6 Docker image with stages setup, build, test, and deploy, and a cache keyed by $CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG that stores the venv directory. In the setup stage add a dependencies job that runs `pip install pipenv`, `if [ ! -d "venv" ]; then virtualenv venv; fi`, checks for Pipfile changes with `git diff --name-only \`cat venv/last_sha || git log --oneline | tail -1 | awk '{print $1}'\` | grep Pipfile; if [ "$? " = "1 " ]; then exit 0; fi`, activates the environment with `. venv/bin/activate`, sets `LC_ALL=C.UTF-8` and `LANG=C.UTF-8`, runs `pipenv install --dev`, and appends the current commit SHA to `venv/last_sha`. In the build stage add a build job that activates the venv (`. venv/bin/activate`) and runs `python setup.py bdist_wheel`, publishes the resulting `dist/*.whl` files as a Build artifact, pulls the venv cache, and runs only on master, release/*, tags, and schedules. In the deploy stage add a deploy job that depends on build, runs `pip install twine` then `twine upload -u $TWINE_USERNAME` to the default PyPI repository, only on master, and a deploy-test job that also depends on build, runs `pip install twine` then `twine upload --repository-url https://test.pypi.org/legacy/ -u $TWINE_USERNAME TerrainLib/*`, only on release/*, both with `GIT_STRATEGY=none` (and `TWINE_PASSWORD=$TWINE_PASSWORD_TEST` for the test job). Also add a pages job that runs only on develop (excluding schedules), installs graphviz with `apt-get update && apt-get install graphviz -y`, activates the venv, generates Sphinx docs via `sphinx-apidoc -o docs_src/api terrainlib` and `make html`, copies the output to `public/`, runs `coverage html` and copies the coverage report to `public/coverage`, caches the venv, depends on test/unit, and publishes the `public/` directory as an HTML Documentation artifact; the test stage contains test/install (installs the built wheel with `pip install `ls dist/TerrainLib*.whl`` and runs a sanity check `python -c "from terrainlib.terrain import Terrain; Terrain(size=512)"`), test/lint (runs `flake8 terrainlib`), and test/unit (runs `coverage run --source terrainlib setup.py test` then after_script `coverage report`, `coverage xml`, and `python-codacy-coverage -r coverage.xml`), each using the venv cache, appropriate allow_failure flags, and only/exclude rules.
